required_complexity_patterns.notes: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click
Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root
At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value
Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs
Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Reference DOM (Display none input): Text input has display:none but label is visible Reference DOM (Split date selects): Start date as three separate day/month/year select elements Reference DOM (Fake role textbox div): Custom component: div with role=textbox, no native input Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: details-accordion, shadow-host, readonly-prefill, pill-yes-no, decoy-form. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Display none input; Split date selects; Fake role textbox div.
